subject: English
chapter: Verghese Kurien—I Too Had A Dream
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – Verghese Kurien—I Too Had A Dream
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Verghese Kurien was an Indian engineer and social entrepreneur who played a major role in transforming India’s dairy industry. He is widely known as the Father of the White Revolution. Through his efforts, India became one of the largest producers of milk in the world.
questions: Who was Verghese Kurien?
What role did he play in India’s dairy industry?
What title is Verghese Kurien known by?
What revolution is associated with Kurien?
What change happened in India because of his efforts?

passages.paragraph: Verghese Kurien was born in Kerala and later studied engineering. His education helped him develop strong technical knowledge and problem-solving skills. These abilities later helped him improve the dairy sector in India.
questions: Where was Verghese Kurien born?
What did he study?
What skills did his education give him?
How did his education help him later?
Which sector benefited from his knowledge?

passages.paragraph: Kurien was posted to Anand in Gujarat to work in a government creamery. At first, he was not interested in dairy work. However, he soon realized the difficulties faced by milk farmers and decided to help them improve their lives.
questions: Where was Kurien posted for work?
What was his job there?
Was he initially interested in dairy work?
What problems did he notice among farmers?
What decision did he make after understanding their problems?

passages.paragraph: Milk farmers often suffered because middlemen bought milk at very low prices. Farmers worked hard but earned very little money. Kurien wanted to change this unfair system and give farmers a better opportunity to earn.
questions: Why did farmers suffer in the dairy trade?
Who bought milk from farmers at low prices?
How much money did farmers earn for their hard work?
What problem did Kurien want to solve?
What opportunity did he want to give farmers?

passages.paragraph: Kurien helped establish a cooperative system where farmers could work together and sell milk collectively. This system ensured that farmers received fair prices for their milk and improved their economic conditions.
questions: What system did Kurien help establish?
How did the cooperative system work?
What benefit did farmers receive from the system?
How did the system improve farmers’ lives?
What was the main purpose of the cooperative system?

passages.paragraph: One of the most successful outcomes of Kurien’s work was the creation of AMUL, a dairy cooperative brand. AMUL became a symbol of India’s dairy success and helped farmers market their milk products efficiently.
questions: What famous dairy brand was created through Kurien’s efforts?
What type of organization is AMUL?
What did AMUL represent for India?
How did AMUL help farmers?
Why did AMUL become successful?

passages.paragraph: Kurien later launched a nationwide program called Operation Flood. This program created a national milk grid that connected rural milk producers with urban markets across India.
questions: What nationwide program did Kurien launch?
What was the purpose of Operation Flood?
What did the national milk grid connect?
Who benefited from this connection?
How did the program help milk distribution?

passages.paragraph: Operation Flood greatly increased milk production in India. Farmers earned better incomes, and milk became widely available to people in cities and villages.
questions: What happened to milk production after Operation Flood?
How did farmers benefit from the program?
Who gained access to more milk?
Did the program help rural or urban people?
What improvement did the program bring to the dairy sector?

passages.paragraph: Verghese Kurien’s work showed the power of cooperation, determination, and innovation. His leadership helped millions of farmers improve their lives and strengthened India’s rural economy.
questions: What qualities did Kurien demonstrate in his work?
How many farmers benefited from his efforts?
What did his leadership strengthen?
How did farmers’ lives change?
What message does his work give about cooperation?

passages.paragraph: The life of Verghese Kurien teaches that dreams combined with dedication and hard work can transform society. His story inspires people to use their knowledge and skills to improve the lives of others.
questions: What does Kurien’s life teach us?
What two qualities helped him achieve his dream?
What kind of change did his work bring to society?
How does his story inspire people?
Why is his story important for students?
